metadata.title: Guideline on the Non-Clinical Development of Fixed Combinations of Medicinal Products
metadata.issuing_body: Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP), European Medicines Agency

rules.rule_text: When there is no experience from use of the combination, even if the individual components are known, bridging studies addressing expected and potential unexpected pharmacodynamic, pharmacokinetic and toxicological interactions are in principle needed.
rules.verbatim_quote: When there is no experience from use of the combination, even if the individual components are known, bridging studies addressing expected and potential unexpected pharmacodynamic, pharmacokinetic and toxicological interactions are in principle needed.
rules.exceptions: Not applicable if there is sufficiently documented human experience of individual and combined use.

rules.rule_text: For any non-clinical combination study, the dose selection should be based on considerations of interspecies differences in pharmacokinetics as well as pharmacodynamics in order to, as close as possible, encompass the clinical situation, both in terms of systemic exposure of animals to the individual components as well as in relation to pharmacodynamic effects while avoiding high-dose non-clinical effects that may be irrelevant to human safety assessment.
rules.verbatim_quote: For any non-clinical combination study, the dose selection should be based on considerations of interspecies differences in pharmacokinetics as well as pharmacodynamics in order to, as close as possible, encompass the clinical situation, both in terms of systemic exposure of animals to the individual components as well as in relation to pharmacodynamic effects while avoiding high-dose non-clinical effects that may be irrelevant to human safety assessment.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The main aim with the non-clinical studies to support the clinical development of a fixed combination is to characterise potential additive, potentiation or antagonistic effects of the compounds when used together with respect to the pharmacology, pharmacokinetics or toxicology of the combination under development.
rules.verbatim_quote: The main aim with the non-clinical studies to support the clinical development of a fixed combination is to characterise potential additive, potentiation or antagonistic effects of the compounds when used together with respect to the pharmacology, pharmacokinetics or toxicology of the combination under development. Additionally, these studies may identify toxicity unique to the combination and previously not seen for either compound administered alone.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The Guideline applies to fixed combinations consisting of chemically defined substances, biological substances or of herbal substances/herbal preparations, and may also be considered in situations of development of a combination therapy not involving a fixed combination formulation. The guideline does not apply to vaccines.
rules.verbatim_quote: The Guideline applies to fixed combinations consisting of chemically defined substances, biological substances or of herbal substances/herbal preparations. The guideline may also be considered in situations of development of a combination therapy not involving a fixed combination formulation. The guideline does not apply to vaccines.
rules.exceptions: Vaccines are explicitly excluded from scope.

rules.rule_text: This document should be read in conjunction with Directive 2001/83/EC (as amended), and relevant CHMP and ICH guidelines.
rules.verbatim_quote: This document should be read in conjunction with Directive 2001/83/EC (as amended), and relevant CHMP and ICH guidelines.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: The extent as well as design of non-clinical studies needed to support development of a fixed combination will depend on the available data for the compounds to be combined, as well as the intended clinical use. Several scenarios are possible: (1) A fixed combination of compounds already approved as free combination therapy; (2) A fixed combination of approved compounds not approved as combination therapy; (3) A fixed combination containing one or more New Active Substances.
rules.verbatim_quote: The extent as well as design of non-clinical studies needed to support development of a fixed combination will depend on the available data for the compounds to be combined, as well as the intended clinical use. Several scenarios are possible: 1. A fixed combination of compounds already approved as free combination therapy. 2. A fixed combination of approved compounds not approved as combination therapy. 3. A fixed combination containing one or more New Active Substances
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: When the fixed combination under development includes compounds for which there is sufficiently documented human experience of their individual and combined use, safety studies in animals are in general not required.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.1 - Fixed combination of compounds already approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: When the fixed combination under development includes compounds for which there is sufficiently documented human experience of their individual and combined use, safety studies in animals are in general not required (CPMP/EWP/240/95).
rules.exceptions: Non-clinical testing may still be warranted when the non-clinical data package does not fulfil the recommendations outlined in the draft Note for Guidance on the Non-Clinical Documentation of Medicinal Products for Mixed Marketing Applications (CPMP/SWP/799/95). In some situations certain non-clinical testing may still be warranted.

rules.rule_text: If the combination contains compounds from the same classes, as other compounds in well established combinations, for which there is considerable clinical experience and there are no pharmacokinetic interactions identified, further non-clinical studies may not be needed. A justification should in such case be provided.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.1 - Fixed combination of compounds already approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: If the combination contains compounds from the same classes, as other compounds in well established combinations, for which there is considerable clinical experience and there are no pharmacokinetic interactions identified, further non-clinical studies may not be needed. A justification should in such case be provided.
rules.exceptions: Justification must be provided in all such cases.

rules.rule_text: For combination of approved compounds not approved as combination therapy, some aspects regarding expected as well as potential unexpected interactions may still need to be addressed. If pharmacodynamic interactions are the rationale for the combination, such effects should be documented. Relevant non-clinical studies may be part of the proof of principle.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Fixed combination of approved compounds NOT approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: For combination of approved compounds not approved as combination therapy, even though the safety and efficacy of the individual substances have been considered adequately documented for approval, some aspects regarding expected as well as potential unexpected interactions may still need to be addressed (CPMP/EWP/240/95). If pharmacodynamic interactions are the rationale for the combination, such effects should be documented. Relevant non-clinical studies may be part of the proof of principle.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacodynamic data with the combination may also be justified to allow assessment of unexpected/undesirable interactions.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Fixed combination of approved compounds NOT approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: Pharmacodynamic data with the combination may also be justified to allow assessment of unexpected/undesirable interactions.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Non-clinical (in vitro or in vivo) or clinical pharmacokinetic interaction studies with the combination should be considered, as appropriate. Provided that the pharmacokinetics of the single components are adequately characterised in animals, including the profile for enzyme induction and inhibition and drug-drug interactions, additional non-clinical documentation on pharmacokinetic interactions is generally not needed.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Fixed combination of approved compounds NOT approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-clinical (in vitro or in vivo) or clinical pharmacokinetic interaction studies with the combination should be considered, as appropriate. Provided that the pharmacokinetics of the single components are adequately characterised in animals, including the profile for enzyme induction and inhibition and drug-drug interactions, additional non-clinical documentation on pharmacokinetic interactions is generally not needed (CPMP/EWP/240/95).
rules.exceptions: If a pharmacokinetic interaction constitutes the rationale for the combination, such interaction should be documented and appropriate non-clinical data may be needed.

rules.rule_text: If a pharmacokinetic interaction constitutes the rationale for the combination, such interaction should be documented and appropriate non-clinical data may be needed.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Fixed combination of approved compounds NOT approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: If a pharmacokinetic interaction constitutes the rationale for the combination, such interaction should be documented and appropriate non-clinical data may be needed.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: For the safety evaluation of the fixed combination, safety pharmacology and toxicity studies should be considered. The need for (combination) studies will depend on the type of the anticipated interactions between the components and on the range of concentrations and exposures covered in the available studies with the single components. If the systemic exposure expected from the use of the fixed combination is not covered adequately by the existing data, additional studies may be warranted.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Fixed combination of approved compounds NOT approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: For the safety evaluation of the fixed combination, safety pharmacology and toxicity studies should be considered. The need for (combination) studies will depend on the type of the anticipated interactions between the components and on the range of concentrations and exposures covered in the available studies with the single components. If the systemic exposure expected from the use of the fixed combination is not covered adequately by the existing data, additional studies may be warranted.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Additional testing may also be justified if the compounds to be combined target the same organ system(s), or belong to a class of compounds associated with a specific type of toxicity. The toxicological profile of the combination may be studied in non-clinical bridging studies, to support the safe human use and identify potential interactions.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Fixed combination of approved compounds NOT approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: Additional testing may also be justified if the compounds to be combined target the same organ system(s), or belong to a class of compounds associated with a specific type of toxicity. The toxicological profile of the combination may be studied in non-clinical bridging studies, to support the safe human use and identify potential interactions.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: In bridging studies, specific endpoints addressing safety concerns based on the characteristics (pharmacology, toxicology) of the individual components may be included in addition to the conventional toxicological evaluation. If sufficient systemic exposure can be obtained, identified relevant safety pharmacology endpoints to be studied may also be included into the (bridging) toxicity studies. In addition, special and/or mechanistic studies may be warranted, e.g. studies addressing immunotoxicity or dependence, depending on the properties of the individual components in the combination.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.2 - Fixed combination of approved compounds NOT approved as free combination therapy
rules.verbatim_quote: In these bridging studies, specific endpoints addressing safety concerns based on the characteristics (pharmacology, toxicology) of the individual components may be included in addition to the conventional toxicological evaluation. If sufficient systemic exposure can be obtained, identified relevant safety pharmacology endpoints to be studied may also be included into the (bridging) toxicity studies. In addition, special and/or mechanistic studies may be warranted, e.g. studies addressing immunotoxicity or dependence, depending on the properties of the individual components in the combination.
rules.exceptions: Contingent on sufficient systemic exposure being obtainable.

rules.rule_text: For New Active Substances developed to be used in a fixed combination or for combined therapy, one approach may be to undertake a complete non-clinical development program with the New Active Substance(s), together with additional bridging studies with the combination, taking the considerations outlined in this document into account. Another approach may be to perform a more extensive non-clinical development program with the combination, together with a limited set of studies with the New Active Substance alone. In each situation, reference to relevant guidance documents should be made.
rules.source_section: Section 4.2.3 - Fixed combination containing one or more New Active Substances
rules.verbatim_quote: For New Active Substances developed to be used in a fixed combination or for combined therapy, different possibilities can be envisaged. One approach may be to undertake a complete non-clinical development program with the New Active Substance(s), together with additional bridging studies with the combination, taking the considerations outlined in this document into account. Another approach may be to perform a more extensive non-clinical development program with the combination, together with a limited set of studies with the New Active Substance alone. In each situation, reference to relevant guidance documents should be made.
rules.exceptions: None stated; choice of approach determined on case-by-case basis.

rules.rule_text: It is desirable that the non-clinical studies are designed in such a way that the exposure range encompasses the anticipated human clinical situation while avoiding high-dose non-clinical effects that may be irrelevant to human safety assessment. Doses in nonclinical studies should be selected to cover for and allow the safe administration of different drug-drug ratios in clinical trials even if only one is ultimately selected for filing and marketing.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is desirable that the non-clinical studies are designed in such a way that the exposure range encompasses the anticipated human clinical situation while avoiding high-dose non-clinical effects that may be irrelevant to human safety assessment. Doses in nonclinical studies should be selected to cover for and allow the safe administration of different drug-drug ratios in clinical trials even if only one is ultimately selected for filing and marketing.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: Deviations from the clinical dose ratios may be necessary if there are marked interspecies differences in the pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic profiles of the compounds. In such a case, adjustment of the dose ratios may be needed to allow appropriate exposure and to avoid irrelevant pharmacodynamic effects in animals masking effects that are relevant for the human safety assessment.
rules.verbatim_quote: Deviations from the clinical dose ratios may be necessary if there are marked interspecies differences in the pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic profiles of the compounds. In such a case, adjustment of the dose ratios may be needed to allow appropriate exposure and to avoid irrelevant pharmacodynamic effects in animals masking effects that are relevant for the human safety assessment.
rules.exceptions: Applicable only when marked interspecies PK/PD differences are present.

rules.rule_text: For general toxicity studies usually one species only may be sufficient. The selection of the species used should be scientifically justified based on pharmacodynamic responsiveness to both components, pharmacokinetics, metabolism, target organs and sensitivity to the toxicological aspects.
rules.verbatim_quote: For general toxicity studies usually one species only may be sufficient. The selection of the species used should be scientifically justified based on pharmacodynamic responsiveness to both components, pharmacokinetics, metabolism, target organs and sensitivity to the toxicological aspects.
rules.exceptions: None stated; one species may suffice if scientifically justified.

rules.rule_text: The need for safety pharmacology studies with the combination will depend on the type of anticipated interactions between the components. The range of concentrations and exposures covered in the available studies with the single components should be taken into consideration. Separate safety pharmacology studies to address specific endpoints (e.g. to study effects on organ systems of concern) may be needed before the initiation of clinical trials with the combination.
rules.verbatim_quote: The need for safety pharmacology studies with the combination will depend on the type of anticipated interactions between the components. The range of concentrations and exposures covered in the available studies with the single components should be taken into consideration. Separate safety pharmacology studies to address specific endpoints (e.g. to study effects on organ systems of concern) may be needed before the initiation of clinical trials with the combination.
rules.exceptions: Existing single-component data may be sufficient if the exposure range is adequately covered.

rules.rule_text: For a fixed combination containing approved compounds indicated for long-term use where experience of concomitant use is lacking, a 3-month repeat dose toxicity study in one appropriate species, supported by toxicokinetic data is generally recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: For a fixed combination containing approved compounds indicated for long-term use where experience of concomitant use is lacking, a 3-month repeat dose toxicity study in one appropriate species, supported by toxicokinetic data is generally recommended.
rules.exceptions: A study of shorter duration may be justified when the intended clinical use is of short duration.

rules.rule_text: The need for studies of longer duration or in an additional species will depend on the effects observed with the combination compared with those induced by the individual components, and on the anticipated pharmacodynamic and/or pharmacokinetic interactions. In cases with complex pharmacodynamic and/or safety profiles, or if there is limited clinical experience with any of the components, further studies may be warranted.
rules.verbatim_quote: The need for studies of longer duration or in an additional species will depend on the effects observed with the combination compared with those induced by the individual components, and on the anticipated pharmacodynamic and/or pharmacokinetic interactions. In cases with complex pharmacodynamic and/or safety profiles, (e.g. targeting of multiple receptors or signalling cascades) or if there is limited clinical experience with any of the components, further studies may be warranted (e.g. mechanistic studies addressing specific endpoints).
rules.exceptions: A study of shorter duration may be justified when the intended clinical use is of short duration.

rules.rule_text: Study designs should be flexible enough to allow as much as possible the comparison with individual components. When the available information with the individual components is insufficient, inclusion in the bridging studies of comparator group(s) treated with the individual components may be needed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Study designs should be flexible enough to allow as much as possible the comparison with individual components. When the available information with the individual components is insufficient, inclusion in the bridging studies of comparator group(s) treated with the individual components may be needed.
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: For fixed combination of non-genotoxic substances, genotoxicity studies with the combination are not needed. If there are concerns regarding genotoxicity for one substance in the combination, further evaluation of e.g. the possibility of a potentiation of the identified effect may be needed. The rationale for addressing such a situation should be assessed on a case-by-case basis taking into consideration the available knowledge of the components and the possible potential of genotoxicity might have on the benefit-risk ratio of the combination in the intended indication.
rules.verbatim_quote: For fixed combination of non-genotoxic substances, genotoxicity studies with the combination are not needed. If there are concerns regarding genotoxicity for one substance in the combination, further evaluation of e.g. the possibility of a potentiation of the identified effect may be needed. The rationale for addressing such a situation should be assessed on a case-by-case basis taking into consideration the available knowledge of the components and the possible potential of genotoxicity might have on the benefit-risk ratio of the combination in the intended indication.
rules.exceptions: Genotoxicity studies not needed if all substances in the combination are non-genotoxic.

rules.rule_text: The carcinogenic potential of a combination should be addressed on the basis of existing information for the individual components. If the fixed combination contains compounds assessed as non-carcinogenic, carcinogenicity studies with the combination would not be needed.
rules.verbatim_quote: The carcinogenic potential of a combination should be addressed on the basis of existing information for the individual components. If the fixed combination contains compounds assessed as non-carcinogenic, carcinogenicity studies with the combination would not be needed.
rules.exceptions: Carcinogenicity studies not needed if all components are assessed as non-carcinogenic.

rules.rule_text: If there is any concern related to one compound in the combination, the risk that this concern increases due to interactions with the additional components should be carefully addressed. If possible and meaningful, it is advisable to include relevant endpoints e.g. for cell proliferation in the bridging repeated dose toxicity study(ies), to obtain further data to address any concern identified.
rules.verbatim_quote: If there is any concern related to one compound in the combination, the risk that this concern increases due to interactions with the additional components, should be carefully addressed. If possible and meaningful, it is advisable to include relevant endpoints e.g. for cell proliferation in the bridging repeated dose toxicity study(ies), to obtain further data to address any concern identified
rules.exceptions: None stated.

rules.rule_text: As for general toxicity testing, comparator groups treated with the individual components may also be included in carcinogenicity studies if existing data are not considered to be sufficient.
rules.verbatim_quote: As for general toxicity testing, comparator groups treated with the individual components may also be included if existing data are not considered to be sufficient.
rules.exceptions: Only required when existing data are not considered sufficient.

rules.rule_text: If the combination contains one New Active Substance, the ideal situation would be that additional animal groups treated with the combination are included into the carcinogenicity study(ies) with the New Active Substance. Deviations from this approach may be acceptable and should be considered on a case-by-case basis.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the combination contains one New Active Substance, the ideal situation would be that additional animal groups treated with the combination are included into the carcinogenicity study(ies) with the New Active Substance. Deviations from this approach may be acceptable and should be considered on a case-by-case basis.
rules.exceptions: Deviations acceptable on a case-by-case basis.

rules.rule_text: When the single components have been adequately tested, and the reproductive/developmental toxicity profiles of these compounds are sufficiently characterised, additional studies with the combination may not be warranted. However, the decision will depend on the nature and properties of the individual components and of their potential interactions.
rules.verbatim_quote: When the single components have been adequately tested, and the reproductive/developmental toxicity profiles of these compounds are sufficiently characterised, additional studies with the combination may not be warranted. However, the decision will depend on the nature and properties of the individual components and of their potential interactions.
rules.exceptions: Additional studies may not be warranted when reproductive/developmental toxicity profiles of all components are sufficiently characterised; case-by-case determination required.

rules.rule_text: When deciding on the timing of studies with a combination in relation to clinical trials, the principles of ICH M3 Guideline will apply. Any deviations from ICH M3 guideline principles should be appropriately justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: When deciding on the timing of studies with a combination in relation to clinical trials the principles of ICH M3 Guideline (CPMP/ICH/286/95: Note for Guidance on Non-Clinical Safety Studies for the Conduct of Human Clinical Trials for Pharmaceuticals) will apply. Any deviations from ICH M3 guideline principles should be appropriately justified.
rules.exceptions: Deviations from ICH M3 principles are permissible only if appropriately justified.
